title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Scale Transfer
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
